result:
Implementation complete. Summary:

- **`model/model.py`**: replaced the single shared-trunk `TinyLocNet` with `SpecialistNet` (from-scratch 4-stage conv trunk, 32→64→96→128 ch, grid_k=16 field+decode+confidence head — no pretrained code, no C4 rotation fan), a trunk-free `Dispatcher` (3 raw-pixel brightness stats → 2-layer MLP → sigmoid gate), and `DispatcherLocNet` that always runs both specialists and blends their outputs (never branches the graph). Added `build_specialist()`, `assemble_dispatcher()`, `specialist_loss_fn()`, `gate_loss_fn()`. `export_onnx`/`estimate_position` contracts unchanged.
- **`model/train.py`**: restructured into three sequential phases — Phase 1 trains a fresh Day specialist (morning/midday/afternoon only, fresh optimizer), Phase 2 trains a completely independent fresh Night specialist (early_evening/evening/night only), Phase 3 freezes both and trains only the ~40-parameter gate across all 6 buckets, then does a final calibration pass on the assembled model's blended confidence. CLI is unchanged.

Verified: syntax check passes, unit-level forward/backward/freeze checks confirm the day/night specialists get zero gradient outside their own phase and only gate params update in phase 3, exported ONNX is 2.48 MiB (well under the 4 MiB gate), and a full smoke-mini-training run on `prignitz` completed end-to-end producing a valid `train_info.json` with the `n_train_crops`/`area`/`epochs`/`device` keys `gallery.py` requires.
